definition: The U.S. economy is in mid-to-late cycle expansion with the exact phase obscured by fiscal support. Stripped of fiscal contributions, private GDP growth is ~1.0-1.5%, consistent with early deceleration, while the private sector surplus of 2-3% GDP makes this more resilient than 1999-2000 or 2005-2007.
evidence: HY OAS 350-420bp (40th-55th percentile), upgrade/downgrade ~0.8x, distressed ratio <5%
Real GDP growth converging on trend
Private sector surplus 2-3% GDP vs deficits in prior late cycles
open_questions: Is fiscal support masking what would otherwise be a recession?
How far advanced is the credit cycle relative to the business cycle?
What is the true stall speed given EBITDA addback distortions?
